name: Carnero En Adobe- Tangy Braised Lamb Shanks
recipeInstructions:
1. Place chilies in a small bowl with boiling water and let stand for 1 hour.
2. Heat lard or oil in a Dutch oven over medium heat until hot. Add 2 lamb shanks. Cook, turning often, until brown on all sides about 20 minutes. Remove to plate. Repeat with remaining lamb shanks.
3. Place chilies and 1/3 cup of the soaking liquid in a blender and process until smooth. Discard remaining soaking liquid.
4. Remove and discard all but 2 tablespoons of oil from the Dutch oven. Add onions and saute over medium heat about 4 minutes or until soft. Reduce heat to medium-low and stir in raisins, sugar, garlic, oregano, and cumin. Add chili puree and cook stirring constantly for 2 minutes.
5. Add tomatoes, stock, and bay leaves to the pan. Bring to a boil. Add lamb shanks and simmer over low heat, covered, turning occasionally until lamb is very tender—about 2 to 2-1/4 hours.
6. Remove lamb to serving platter and keep warm. Skim and discard fat from cooking sauce. Stir in vinegar. Heat sauce to boiling over medium-high heat and cook uncovered, stirring frequently until sauce is thickened—about 10 minutes. Spoon sauce over lamb. Serve garnished with lettuce and olives (and maybe even some quartered tomatoes). Accompany with Refried Beans garnished as desired with shredded cheese and diced white onions. It is recommended to clean the shanks thoroughly of as much fat as possible, but do not remove all the membrane; it holds them together.
